description_long: This electric fan features a robust, industrial design with a spherical black metal housing at its center, supporting two large sets of golden, four-bladed fans on either side. The fan blades are encased in intricate, geometric wire guards made of gold-colored metal, providing both protection and a visually striking appearance. The base is solid and heavy, likely cast iron or steel, with a visible rotary control at the front and a power cord extending from the rear. The fan's overall construction suggests durability and functionality, with exposed screws and mechanical joints adding to its utilitarian aesthetic. The power cord is thick and dark, ending in a standard two-prong plug, suitable for urban electrical outlets. The combination of metallic finishes and angular forms gives the fan a vintage, steampunk-inspired look, making it a distinctive fixture in urban environments such as workshops, garages, or industrial lofts.
description: A vintage-style electric fan with dual golden blade sets, geometric wire guards, and a sturdy black metal base, featuring a visible power cord and rotary control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the golden fan blades and the geometric wire guard, with the central black housing and part of the base visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the spherical black housing, both sets of fan blades, and the full extent of the wire guards, with the power cord and plug clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the opposite set of golden blades and wire guard, with the central housing and base partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the rotary control on the base, both sets of blades, and the wire guards, with the power cord extending from the rear.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is a geometric open frame made of gold-colored metal rods, forming an angular, cage-like structure around the blades for protection.
